ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT code identified for sandwich biscuit with specialty filling; SNOMED CT coverage for processed snack subcategories is limited.

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical single serving of sandwich biscuits is approximately 30 g, equivalent to about three biscuit sandwiches or one small snack packet.

nutrient_source.title: Sandwich Biscuit (Other Filling) — Singapore HPB Food Composition Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values taken directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for 'Sandwich biscuit (other filling)', CR ID F11080057. All core macros and secondary nutrients are sourced from this record at confidence 0.95.

gi_source.title: Biscuits and cookies GI values — University of Sydney GI Database; analogous estimate for sandwich biscuit with cream filling
gi_source.source_match_notes: GI of 70 (high) is estimated from the University of Sydney GI Database entry for cream-filled sandwich biscuits / sweet biscuits, which report GI values in the range 65–75. A midpoint value of 70 is applied. Glycemic load per serving calculated as (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (70 × 19.85) / 100 ≈ 13.9 for a 30 g serving (19.85 g carbohydrate).

hypertension.key_factor: Very high sugar (30.57 g/100 g) with negligible dietary fibre (0.75 g/100 g) drives insulin-mediated sodium retention; sodium at 291 mg/100 g is also moderate
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should avoid this snack; the very high sugar content (30.57 g/100 g) independently elevates blood pressure risk through insulin-mediated sodium retention, and the moderate sodium load (291 mg/100 g) compounds cardiovascular strain.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Sugar 30.57 g/100 g far exceeds the 20 g/100 g glycaemic ceiling; high GI (70) and high carbohydrate (66.15 g/100 g) with minimal fibre (0.75 g/100 g) cause rapid post-meal glucose spikes
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should avoid this product entirely; the combination of very high sugar (30.57 g/100 g), high GI (70), and negligible dietary fibre (0.75 g/100 g) will cause significant and rapid post-meal blood glucose elevation.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High saturated fat (~11.5 g/100 g from palm oil), very high sugar (30.57 g/100 g), and ultra-processed formulation with likely trans fat residues are all atherogenic
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should avoid this snack; the high saturated fat content (~11.5 g/100 g), very high sugar (30.57 g/100 g), and ultra-processed nature of this product collectively raise LDL cholesterol and cardiovascular risk.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Very high refined sugar (30.57 g/100 g) promotes pro-inflammatory mediators; palm oil-based saturated fat exceeds the 5 g/100 g pro-inflammatory threshold
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: People with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid this snack; the very high refined sugar and saturated fat content promote systemic inflammation and may worsen joint symptoms.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Sandwich Biscuit (Other Filling) is an ultra-processed sweet snack consisting of two baked wheat-flour biscuit wafers sandwiched around a specialty cream or flavoured filling. The biscuits are crisp and lightly salted, while the filling is sweet, dense, and fat-rich, creating a contrasting texture. This product category is widely sold in Singapore school canteens, convenience stores, and supermarkets under various brand names. The filling type is unspecified ('other filling'), distinguishing it from vanilla or chocolate cream variants. Per 100 g, this snack delivers 506 kcal, 5.77 g protein, 24.3 g fat (predominantly saturated fat from palm oil, approximately 11.5 g), 66.15 g carbohydrate, and 30.57 g sugar — making it one of the highest-sugar biscuit products in the HPB Singapore food database. Dietary fibre is negligible at 0.75 g/100 g. Sodium is moderate at 291 mg/100 g. The glycemic index is estimated at 70 (high), and a 30 g serving delivers a glycemic load of approximately 13.9. Clinically, this product is unsuitable for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes, hypertension, cardiovascular disease, or rheumatoid arthritis due to its very high sugar, high saturated fat, high GI, and ultra-processed formulation. It should be treated as an occasional indulgence only and avoided by those with metabolic or inflammatory conditions.
common_user_queries: How many calories in a sandwich biscuit?
Is sandwich biscuit high in sugar?
What is the GI of sandwich biscuits?
Is sandwich cookie suitable for hypertension?

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database (CR ID F11080057) at confidence 0.95. This is an exact match from the primary approved regional source for Singapore food records. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for the sandwich biscuit / sweet biscuit category.
notes: iron_mg is 0.0 per the HPB source (F11080057); this is a confirmed zero value as explicitly reported by the approved source. saturated_fat_g of 11.5 g/100 g is an adapted estimate based on the known palm oil fat profile typical of this biscuit category, as the HPB entry does not separately itemise saturated fat; this is documented as an adapted value. The following fields are set to -1 because the detailed data is not reported in the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry F11080057 and is not available in any other approved source consulted (HPB Foodo, USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy) for this specific sandwich biscuit category as of 2026: monounsaturated_fat_g — fatty acid fractionation not reported by HPB for this product category; polyunsaturated_fat_g — fatty acid fractionation not reported by HPB for this product category; trans_fat_g — trans fat not separately reported by HPB for this product category; potassium_mg — not reported in HPB F11080057 entry; cholesterol_mg — not reported in HPB F11080057 entry; vitamin_c_mg — not reported in HPB F11080057 entry; vitamin_a_mcg_rae — not reported in HPB F11080057 entry; vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg — not reported in HPB F11080057 entry; vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg — not reported in HPB F11080057 entry; vitamin_b3_niacin_mg — not reported in HPB F11080057 entry; vitamin_b6_mg — not reported in HPB F11080057 entry; vitamin_b12_mcg — not reported in HPB F11080057 entry; folate_mcg_dfe — not reported in HPB F11080057 entry; vitamin_d_mcg — not reported in HPB F11080057 entry; vitamin_e_mg — not reported in HPB F11080057 entry; phosphorus_mg — not reported in HPB F11080057 entry; magnesium_mg — not reported in HPB F11080057 entry; zinc_mg — not reported in HPB F11080057 entry; selenium_mcg — not reported in HPB F11080057 entry; water_g — moisture content not reported in HPB F11080057 entry. This is a Western-origin generic packaged snack; no mandatory local-language names (Chinese, Malay, Tamil, Hindi) are required as this food is not a Singaporean dish — it is a globally distributed Western processed snack product catalogued in the Singapore HPB database. Multilingual local_names fields are therefore null by design and G6 is satisfied. FoodOn URI and FoodEx2 code are applied as defensible analogous matches for the sandwich cookie / filled biscuit category.
